soap:
SOAP · lance-format/lance#7574 — fix(index): exclude shared metadata cache from LanceIndexStore deep_size

Subjective
  Ali2Arslan (infrequent contributor, Cursor-assisted) removes self.metadata_cache.deep_size_of_children(context) from LanceIndexStore::deep_size_of_children. Motivation: metadata_cache is an Arc<LanceCache> session-scoped and shared across every index store in a session; Session::deep_size_of_children already accounts for it once, and because LanceCache::deep_size_of_children returns approx_size_bytes() without registering in the dedup Context, every store that also sizes it re-counts the entire cache, N-times inflating reported memory. Adds a regression test (4 MB blob; 276 vs 4,194,620 bytes pre-fix).

Objective
  - lance_format.rs:39-50 — metadata_cache: Arc<LanceCache>, confirming shared ownership.
  - lance_format.rs:52-61 — one-line removal; sums only object_store and index_dir now.
  - Verified against live repo at head SHA: (a) production call sites construct LanceIndexStore with the dataset's session-owned Arc<LanceCache>, not a private instance; (b) Session::deep_size_of_children already sums this same cache; (c) LanceCache's DeepSizeOf calls approx_size_bytes() and does NOT use Context for pointer dedup — confirming the double-count mechanism.
  - Test test_store_deep_size_excludes_metadata_cache (610-648): real LanceIndexStore + shared cache, snapshot, insert 4 MB blob, force commit via size_bytes().await, assert store size unchanged. Genuine regression test that would fail pre-fix.
  - object_store: Arc<ObjectStore> is also potentially shared and still summed unconditionally; whether ObjectStore's DeepSizeOf participates in Context dedup NOT checked — out of scope, flagged as similar-shaped risk.
  - CI green, mergeable, single file +44/-1, no omitted files.

Assessment
  risk: low-to-med (down from baseline med) — pure accounting change (deep_size_of/memory telemetry), not data path/IO/concurrency/format. A wrong answer misleads memory metrics/eviction heuristics but doesn't corrupt data or panic.
  urgency: low · escalation: none · could not read: none
  Fix is correct and directly verified against the shared-cache architecture, but it is a point patch on a systemic issue: LanceCache::deep_size_of_children not participating in Context dedup means the same bug can resurface at any other holder of a shared Arc<LanceCache>. Worth a non-blocking root-cause ask.

Plan
  No blocking conditions. Non-blocking: fix the root cause in LanceCache's DeepSizeOf (Context-based dedup) so double-counting is fixed for every holder at once; sanity-check object_store for the same shape.

RECOMMENDATION: approve
summary: Correct, narrowly-scoped fix: LanceIndexStore no longer double-counts the shared, session-scoped metadata_cache in deep_size_of_children. Verified against the live repo that the cache is genuinely shared across all index stores in a session and that Session already accounts for it once. Regression test is real and demonstrates the pre-fix bug with concrete before/after numbers. Non-blocking: the root cause (LanceCache::deep_size_of_children ignoring the dedup Context) is untouched, so the same double-counting can recur at any other future holder of the shared cache.

comments.label: suggestion
comments.body: suggestion: This fixes the symptom at this one call site. The actual root cause is that LanceCache::deep_size_of_children returns approx_size_bytes() without registering itself against the dedup Context — so any other current or future holder of the same shared Arc<LanceCache> will double-count it the same way. Consider a follow-up (or doing it here) to make LanceCache's DeepSizeOf impl participate in Context-based dedup, fixing double-counting for every holder at once rather than requiring each call site to remember to exclude it.

comments.label: praise
comments.body: praise: Good regression test — it builds a real LanceIndexStore with a live shared cache, inserts a blob, and asserts the store's reported size doesn't move. A genuine before/after test (fails pre-fix with a concrete byte count), not just an assertion pinned to the fixed behavior.

comments.label: question
comments.body: question: object_store: Arc<ObjectStore> is also potentially shared (across stores within a session or dataset). Does ObjectStore's DeepSizeOf participate in the dedup Context, or could it have the same double-counting problem this PR just fixed for metadata_cache? Not asking you to fix it here — flagging the same shape of risk in the line right above the one you removed.
